title: Manifesto
description: Why family life needs shared operational infrastructure.

sections.title: Invisible cognitive load is real
sections.body: Household operations include schedules, school logistics, meals, documents, contracts, maintenance, finances, and care tasks. Much of this remains undocumented and concentrated in one person.

sections.title: Mental labor is uneven by default
sections.body: When memory lives in one person’s head, workload distribution is invisible. The result is stress, imbalance, and brittle coordination.

sections.title: Households lack infrastructure
sections.body: Teams and companies use operational systems. Households typically do not. Families need infrastructure designed for shared clarity, continuity, and anticipation.

sections.title: Shared operational memory
sections.body: DomusMind models people, responsibilities, timeline, routines, assets, documents, properties, inventory, pets, and logistics as durable household state.

sections.title: The vision
sections.body: DomusMind is open platform infrastructure for family operations: API-first, domain-centric, event-driven, and self-hostable. It evolves from coordination to anticipation and automation.
